title: sloanea guianensis subsp. stipitata
intro: Sloanea guianensis subsp. stipitata, also known as the Stipitate Sloanea, is a plant species found in the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Sloanea guianensis subsp. stipitata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against infections, as well as its ability to support the immune system and reduce oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are typically made from the leaves, bark, or roots of the plant.
